audit_status.source_base: Greek James text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for desires/conflict, friendship with world, humility, judging, planning, arrogance.

verses.restored: From where are wars and from where fights among you? Are they not from here, from your pleasures/desires warring in your members?
You desire and do not have; you murder and envy and cannot obtain; you fight and war. You do not have because you do not ask.
You ask and do not receive because you ask wrongly, so that you may spend it in your pleasures.
Adulterous ones, do you not know that friendship with the world is hostility toward the Divine? Therefore whoever wills to be friend of the world is made enemy of the Divine.
Or do you suppose Scripture speaks emptily? The Spirit he made dwell in us yearns with jealousy.
But he gives greater grace; therefore it says, The Divine opposes arrogant ones but gives grace to humble ones.
Therefore be arranged under the Divine; resist the adversarial one, and he will flee from you.
Draw near to the Divine, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse hands, sinners/misaligned ones, and purify hearts, double-souled ones.
Lament and mourn and weep; let your laughter be turned into mourning and your joy into heaviness.
Be humbled before the Lord, and he will lift you.
Do not speak against one another, siblings. The one speaking against a sibling or judging one's sibling speaks against Law and judges Law; but if you judge Law, you are not doer of Law but judge.
One is Lawgiver and Judge, the One able to save/heal and destroy; but who are you, the one judging the neighbor?
Come now, those saying, Today or tomorrow we will go into this city and spend a year there and trade and make gain,
you who do not know what tomorrow brings. What is your life? For you are vapor appearing for a little and then disappearing.
Instead you ought to say, If the Lord wills, we will both live and do this or that.
But now you boast in your arrogant pretensions; all such boasting is harmful.
Therefore to the one knowing to do beautiful/good and not doing it, to that one it is sin/misalignment.

translation_notes: Conflict is traced to disordered desire and grasping, not to embodied desire as inherently evil.
Humility and repentance are restorative movements, not shame as identity.
Planning critique targets arrogant self-certainty, not responsible preparation.
